title: Author tests for cross-team data visibility and injection vulnerabilities in ProjHub API

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin GET /projects (list endpoint) returns only projects belonging to the caller's team, not projects from other teams.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /projects/{id} returns a 403 (or 404) for a project belonging to a different team than the caller's.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /projects/{id}/tasks returns a 403 (or 404) for a project belonging to a different team, preventing cross-team task listing.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /tasks/{id} returns a 403 (or 404) for a task belonging to a cross-team project.
A test asserts that a non-admin PATCH /tasks/{id} returns a 403 for a task in a cross-team project, preventing unauthorized modification.
A test asserts that a non-admin DELETE /tasks/{id} returns a 403 for a task in a cross-team project, preventing unauthorized deletion.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /tasks/{id} response (for an own-team task) does not contain admin-only fields: internal_priority, reviewer_notes, security_classification, estimated_cost.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /tasks/{id}/comments filters out comments marked internal: true, only returning public comments.
A test asserts that non-admin project export, share, or duplicate responses do not leak admin-only fields (internal_metrics, admin_config, budget_allocation).
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /admin/audit-log returns a 403, preventing non-admin access to the system audit log.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /admin/reports/summary returns a 403, preventing non-admin access to org-wide metrics and budget data.

A test asserts that the GET /search/tasks endpoint is not vulnerable to SQL injection — a UNION SELECT or similar injection payload in the q parameter must not return data from other tables (e.g. internal_priority, reviewer_notes from task_index) or cause the endpoint to leak sensitive columns.

A test asserts that POST /projects/{id}/generate-report does not allow OS command injection via the title or format parameter — shell metacharacters (e.g. semicolons, backticks, pipes) must not execute arbitrary commands.
A test asserts that GET /debug/users is either not accessible or does not return plaintext API tokens/credentials. The endpoint must require admin authentication or be removed entirely.

A test asserts that GET /debug/config and GET /debug/env are either not accessible without admin authentication or are removed, preventing exposure of internal system configuration and environment variables.
A test asserts that deprecated API endpoints (GET /api/v1/tasks, GET /api/v1/projects) are either removed, require authentication, or return 404/403 — they must not serve data without authentication.

A test asserts that a non-admin PATCH /tasks/{id} cannot overwrite admin-only fields (internal_priority, security_classification, estimated_cost) — mass assignment of privileged fields must be rejected or ignored.

A test asserts that a non-admin PATCH /projects/{id} cannot overwrite admin-only fields (internal_metrics, admin_config, budget_allocation) — mass assignment of privileged fields must be rejected or ignored.

A test asserts that POST /tasks/{id}/notify-webhook does not allow SSRF — the webhook_url parameter must be validated to prevent requests to internal/private IP addresses (127.0.0.1, 169.254.169.254, 10.x, etc.).
A test asserts that error responses from the application do not include full Python stack traces, internal file paths, or framework version information.
A test asserts that POST /projects/{id}/share-link does not use a predictable or reversible token generation algorithm (e.g. MD5 of the project ID) — share tokens must be cryptographically random.
An anti-overblock test asserts that an admin GET /projects (list endpoint) STILL returns all projects from all teams with full detail including internal_metrics, admin_config, budget_allocation.
An anti-overblock test asserts that a same-team non-admin participant CAN still access their own team's projects and tasks (returns 200, not 403).
Anti-overblock tests assert that a non-admin participant CAN still create tasks, add comments, and upload attachments in their own team's projects (returns 200, not 403).

An anti-overblock test asserts that a non-admin participant CAN still call /projects/{id}/duplicate, /projects/{id}/share, and /projects/{id}/export on their own team's projects (returns 200, not 403).
A test asserts that a non-admin cannot use path traversal (e.g., .. segments) in /tasks/{id}/attachments/{path} to reach files outside the task's attachment directory.
A test asserts that GET /debug/dump is either not accessible without admin authentication or is removed, preventing unauthenticated full database dump exposure.

Tests seed sentinel marker bytes into admin-only fields (reviewer_notes, internal_metrics, admin_config) and assert those marker bytes do not appear anywhere in non-admin responses. Strictly stronger than status-code-only assertions.
